text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
While passing by the train station on our way
home, Hishi Akebono is handed a flyer for a
planetarium. Apparently, an expert in space
science will be commentating on today's show.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Wow, we can learn all about stars and space
there! And look, the flyer comes with discount
coupons.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text: Yes, please! It sounds really fun! ♪

text_block_list.name: Marvelous Sunday
text_block_list.text:
Ohhh! Hello there, Akebono! How marvelous to
see you here! ☆

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Hi, Marvelous! What a coincidence! Are you here
to look at the stars too?

text_block_list.name: Marvelous Sunday
text_block_list.text:
But of course. ☆ I'm in search of marvelous and
sparkly things. ★

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Oh, I hope you can find some. Hey, looks like the
show's starting!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
The night sky spreads out across the domed
ceiling above us. The commentary covers not only
the projected constellations, but also the
composition of stars and the Milky Way.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text: There are so many stars in the universe...

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Hey, I wonder what aliens eat... Do you think they
can eat Earth food?

text_block_list.name: Woman A
text_block_list.text:
Pfft. I can't believe there are people who actually
believe in aliens.

text_block_list.name: Man A
text_block_list.text:
She probably thinks they're real 'cause she
watched some sci-fi flick. How gullible can you
get?

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Yeah... People have never seen aliens, so it's no
wonder they don't believe in them, huh. Oh well!

text_block_list.name: Marvelous Sunday
text_block_list.text:
There are quite a few terrestrial-type planets
throughout the Milky Way Galaxy that could be
considered habitable, and it's possible that some
of them have water.

text_block_list.name: Marvelous Sunday
text_block_list.text:
So it wouldn't be a stretch to say we could find
life-forms in those environments! Space is still full
of mysteries, so who knows what could happen!
How marvelous! ☆ Galactically marvelous! ★☆

text_block_list.name: Man A
text_block_list.text: H-hey, let's get out of here...

text_block_list.name: Space Science Commentator
text_block_list.text:
Hehe. I'm pleased to see all this enthusiasm you
have for space. If you'd like, I could answer your
earlier question.

text_block_list.name: Space Science Commentator
text_block_list.text: While we don't know what aliens eat... it is possible that Earth food would be edible for them.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Really?! In that case, I'd love to have some
chanko with them!

text_block_list.name: Space Science Commentator
text_block_list.text:
Eating chanko might prove a bit difficult. The
soup and ingredients would float all over the
place in a zero-gravity environment, you see.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text: What?! They wouldn't just stay together?!

text_block_list.name: Space Science Commentator
text_block_list.text:
No. That's why all space foods and drinks are
made to be viscous. The surface tension helps
keep everything together.

text_block_list.name: Marvelous Sunday
text_block_list.text:
How does space food taste? Is it delicious? Is it...
marvelous?

text_block_list.name: Space Science Commentator
text_block_list.text:
You know, I think it's become quite delicious,
actually. There are a lot of researchers looking
into how to make space food tastier for
astronauts.

text_block_list.name: Marvelous Sunday
text_block_list.text:
Marvelous! ☆ That's just like your food, Akebono!
Your delicious dishes make everyone feel
marvelous too!

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
You're so right. I wonder if space food is made by
people who want other people to enjoy yummy
stuff too?

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text: I want to learn more about space food now!
choice_data_list: Do you want to try making some?
Let's buy some and try it!

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text: Yes! I really want to try making space chanko!

text_block_list.name: Space Science Commentator
text_block_list.text:
In that case, I think watching some videos of
astronauts eating space food would make for
good reference material. They often review the
food too.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
We immediately watch some videos during our
break. I'm grateful that they're subtitled so we
can watch them even in a place like this.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Whoa. So in space, water breaks apart like that?!
This must be what that scientist meant about the
chanko going everywhere!

text_block_list.name: Marvelous Sunday
text_block_list.text:
Ooh! When the food's canned, the contents stay
in place even when you turn it upside-down!
Marvelous! This stuff looks like it'd be hard to
mix, though.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Yeah, everything floats when there's no gravity,
so I guess it's impossible to mix anything...
Cooking in space sounds really tough!

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Wow... This astronaut just said she really likes
Japanese food, and she looks forward to
Japanese dishes the most at mealtimes.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
I bet she'd be over the moon if she could try some
hot pot...

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
I'm going to try to create a recipe for astronaut
chanko! If I make it and upload it to Umasta,
maybe it'll inspire space food researchers!

text_block_list.name: Marvelous Sunday
text_block_list.text:
How marvelous! ☆ Let's do it! ★ We've got to do
our best to get chanko into space! ☆

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Yeah! Imagine if we could sit and share chanko
with astronauts and aliens someday!

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Oh, they're really selling it! You can buy space
food from the souvenir corner!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
We buy the space food versions of both curry rice
and miso-stewed chicken to try back on campus.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text: Okay, let's have them now!

text_block_list.name: Marvelous Sunday
text_block_list.text:
The curry rice is marvelous! ☆ The spices are
really intense. ★

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
The miso-stewed chicken tastes pretty salty. And
they've really thickened the soup stock.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Apparently, space food is purposefully
over-seasoned because zero-gravity
environments dull people's sense of taste.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I also heard that space food is produced with
reduced salt content and higher nutritional value
to make it healthier for astronauts.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
I had no idea so much thought went into making
space food... It's really made with the astronauts
in mind...

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text: People worked super hard to come up with these flavors...

text_block_list.name: Marvelous Sunday
text_block_list.text:
Isn't it maaarvelous when things taste
delicious? ☆★☆

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Yeah. And this has really reminded me taste isn't
the only thing that makes food delicious.
